query: US Iran ceasefire negotiations June 2026 60-day window Trump
answer:
The research agent found extensive information from pre-existing research files. This is a very comprehensive picture. Let me compile the final report.

---

# US-Iran Ceasefire Negotiations: June 2026 — The 60-Day Window and Trump's Role

## Executive Summary

The US and Iran are on the verge of signing a landmark deal to end the **2026 Iran War**. On **June 14, 2026**, President Trump announced the deal is "complete." A **signing ceremony is scheduled for June 19, 2026, in Geneva, Switzerland**, with VP JD Vance expected to attend. A **60-day ceasefire extension** was announced on **June 11, 2026**, to finalize all remaining issues. However, as of June 15–16, **no formal agreement has yet been signed**, and significant risks remain.

---

## The 2026 Iran War: Background

- **February 28, 2026**: The US and Israel launched **"Operation Midnight Hammer"** — coordinated strikes that **killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei** and destroyed nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan. Over 11,000 targets struck; 3,000+ Iranians killed.
- **Mojtaba Khamenei** (son of the deceased leader) was selected as the new Supreme Leader on March 8–9, 2026.
- Iran retaliated and **closed the Strait of Hormuz** — described by S&P Global as "the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy."

---

## The 60-Day Ceasefire Window

- **June 11, 2026**: Trump announced a **60-day ceasefire extension** with Iran to negotiate all remaining contentious issues for a final agreement.
- This extends an earlier ceasefire brokered by **Pakistan on April 7–8** (initially a two-week ceasefire), then extended indefinitely by Trump on **April 21, 2026**.
- The 60-day window is intended to provide time for finalizing and implementing all terms of the deal, including the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

---

## Trump's Direct Involvement

| Date | Action |
|------|--------|
| **May 17** | Set 5 preconditions: Iran delivers 400 kg enriched uranium to US; maintain only one nuclear facility; negotiations tied to cessation of hostilities; refusal to release 25%+ of frozen assets; rejection of reparations demand |
| **May 24** | Said MOU was "close to finalization" |
| **Jun 6** | Announced VP Vance would attend signing "in Europe" |
| **Jun 10–11** | After two nights of US-Iran strike exchanges (including threats against Kharg Island), called off strikes and announced 60-day ceasefire extension |
| **Jun 14** | Announced deal is "complete"; authorized removal of US naval blockade |

- Defense Secretary Hegseth defended the June 10–11 strikes as "negotiate with bombs."
- Wikipedia notes Trump "falsely claimed over 30 times since March" that a deal was near, warranting some caution.

---

## Full Negotiation Timeline

| Date | Event |
|------|-------|
| Feb 6 | Round 1 of 2026 talks (Muscat, Oman) |
| Feb 15 | Round 2 (Geneva) |
| Feb 25–26 | Round 3 — agreement "within reach" (Geneva) |
| **Feb 28** | **War begins — Operation Midnight Hammer** |
| Apr 7–8 | Two-week ceasefire brokered by Pakistan |
| Apr 11–12 | **Islamabad Talks** — first direct US-Iran talks since 1979; 21 hours; collapsed |
| Apr 13 | US imposes naval blockade on Iranian ports |
| Apr 21 | Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely |
| May 2 | Iran submits 14-point ceasefire proposal (rejected) |
| May 4–5 | Major escalation: Iran attacks UAE; US sinks 7 IRGC boats |
| May 17 | Trump sets 5 preconditions |
| May 22 | Qatar joins Pakistan as co-mediator |
| May 24 | Trump says MOU "close to finalization" |
| Jun 6 | Trump announces Vance to attend signing |
| Jun 10–11 | Strike exchanges; Trump announces 60-day ceasefire extension |
| **Jun 14** | **Trump announces deal is "complete"** |
| **Jun 19** | **Signing ceremony scheduled — Geneva** |

---

## Key Reported Deal Terms (as of June 14–15)

- Strait of Hormuz to **reopen immediately without tolls**
- **Full restoration of pre-war shipping within ~30 days**
- Ceasefire extension provisions
- **Nuclear and sanctions terms NOT yet publicly disclosed**
- Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed the deal; Pakistan PM Sharif confirmed deal text was agreed upon (AP, June 13)

---

## Key Negotiators

| Side | Key Figures |
|------|-------------|
| **US** | VP JD Vance, Envoys Steve Witkoff & Jared Kushner, Admiral Brad Cooper |
| **Iran** | FM Abbas Araghchi, Deputy FM Gharibabadi, Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf |
| **Mediators** | Pakistan (PM Sharif, FM Asim Munir — primary), Qatar (co-mediator from May 22) |

---

## Major Risks to the Deal

1. **IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal** — critical given their operational control of the Strait of Hormuz
2. **Israeli opposition** — Netanyahu struck Beirut on June 14; Israel's Security Cabinet may formally oppose; Israel feels "sidelined"
3. **Internal Iranian fragility** — New Supreme Leader's authority not consolidated; civilian-military rifts
4. **Congressional opposition** — 52 senators and 177 representatives signed letter opposing any deal allowing continued enrichment
5. **Secretary Rubio's skepticism**: "I'm not sure you can reach a deal with these guys"
6. **Trump credibility concerns** — repeated premature claims of imminent deals since March
7. **Market skepticism** — Fitch assessed broad Hormuz reopening as unlikely before July

---

## Economic Impact

| Metric | Value | Date |
|--------|-------|------|
| Brent crude (pre-war) | ~$72.50/bbl | Feb 27 |
| Brent crude (peak) | $126.10/bbl | Late Apr |
| Brent crude (post-deal announcement) | ~$83.63/bbl | Jun 15 |
| US gasoline (pre-war) | $2.98/gal | Feb 26 |
| US gasoline (peak) | $4.45/gal (+49%) | May 3 |
| Global oil supply loss | ~2 million bpd | May |
| Iran daily blockade loss | $435 million/day | Apr–Jun |
| US war cost | ~$25–29 billion | Apr–May |
| Trump approval rating | Below 30% | May |

---

## Prediction Market Signals (as of June 15, 2026)

| Market | Question | Price |
|--------|----------|-------|
| Metaculus #44056 | US & Iran sign agreement before June 26? | **75%** |
| Metaculus #44050 | Iran reopen Strait of Hormuz before June 27? | **40%** |
| Polymarket | US and Iran sign agreement by Jul 31 | **~81.5%** |

---

key_facts: On June 11, 2026, Trump announced a 60-day ceasefire extension with Iran to finalize remaining deal terms, extending earlier ceasefires dating back to April 7-8 (brokered by Pakistan).
On June 14, 2026, Trump announced the deal is 'complete' and authorized removal of the US naval blockade; Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed from the Iranian side.
A signing ceremony is scheduled for June 19, 2026, in Geneva, Switzerland, with VP JD Vance expected to attend.
The war began on February 28, 2026, with 'Operation Midnight Hammer' — US-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and destroyed nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan.
Iran retaliated by closing the Strait of Hormuz, described by S&P Global as 'the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy.'

Trump set 5 preconditions on May 17: Iran delivers 400 kg enriched uranium, maintains only one nuclear facility, ties negotiations to cessation of hostilities, refusal to release 25%+ of frozen assets, rejection of reparations.
The IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal — a critical risk given their operational control of the Strait of Hormuz.
Key mediators: Pakistan (primary, brokered initial ceasefire) and Qatar (co-mediator from May 22).
Key US negotiators: VP JD Vance, Special Envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, Admiral Brad Cooper.
52 US senators and 177 representatives signed a letter opposing any deal allowing continued enrichment.
Wikipedia notes Trump 'falsely claimed over 30 times since March' that a deal was near, warranting caution about the June 14 announcement.
The Islamabad Talks (April 11-12) were the first direct US-Iran talks since 1979, lasting 21 hours but collapsing without a deal.
Brent crude peaked at $126.10/bbl in late April from ~$72.50 pre-war, settling to ~$83.63 after the deal announcement on June 15.
US gasoline peaked at $4.45/gal (+49% from pre-war $2.98/gal) on May 3.
As of June 15, Polymarket prices US-Iran agreement by July 31 at ~81.5%; Metaculus prices signing before June 26 at 75%.
